name: Varaha

visual_attributes.morphology: Anthropomorphic form with a boar's head and human body
distinctive_features: Often depicted lifting the earth goddess Bhumi
visual_attributes.texture: Rough, bristly fur on the boar's head
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: Gigantic, capable of lifting the earth
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Boar's head seamlessly integrated with a muscular human torso

domain_specific_visual.CROWN: Often depicted wearing a crown symbolizing divinity
domain_specific_visual.WEAPON: Carries a mace or discus as a symbol of power

functional_attributes.primary_function: Rescuer of the earth goddess Bhumi

domain_specific_functional.ROLE_IN_MYTH: Restores cosmic order by defeating the demon Hiranyaksha

relationships.description: Varaha lifts and rescues Bhumi, the earth goddess
Varaha defeats the demon Hiranyaksha to rescue the earth
Varaha is the third avatar of the Hindu god Vishnu

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Represents the divine intervention to restore balance and order in the universe

contrastive_constraints: NOT unrelated to Vishnu or Hindu mythology
